Reduce heap allocations in hot paths

  the base path then stamps one extra key, avoiding path Vec allocation.
  Factor shared walk() helper out of stamp/stamp_child.

  ItemsView __contains__ (zero-copy borrow from Python string).

  in require_int_key error path.
- Eliminate intermediate Vecs in __iter__: Document, KeysView,
  ValuesView, and ItemsView build PyList directly from iterators
  via for_each_key callback. item_keys reuses for_each_key.

commit.message:
build: embed RPATH to dependencies in installed targets (#317)

* build: embed RPATH to dependencies in installed targets

Set CMAKE_INSTALL_RPATH_USE_LINK_PATH=TRUE so CMake automatically
appends the link-time library directories of all linked targets
(Geant4, OpenCASCADE, …) to the RPATH of every installed binary and
shared library.  Without this, the installed g4occt executable and
libG4OCCT.so had no RPATH, requiring LD_LIBRARY_PATH to be set
manually at runtime.

The two companion variables are set explicitly for clarity:
  CMAKE_SKIP_BUILD_RPATH       FALSE  (keep RPATH in build tree)
  CMAKE_BUILD_WITH_INSTALL_RPATH FALSE (use build-tree paths during build)

cmake-format also reflowed three pre-existing long lines that exceeded
the 100-column limit (no functional change).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eic/G4OCCT/sessions/bedf1a56-006f-4e42-ad0c-e5406c5df797

* Address code review: fix docstring, simplify try-catch, remove redundant Geant4_LIBRARIES, consolidate logging

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eic/G4OCCT/sessions/bedf1a56-006f-4e42-ad0c-e5406c5df797

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix DD4hep plugin: compile with C++17 to match DD4hep container headers

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eic/G4OCCT/sessions/6e794f34-c07c-4ca9-ad92-ccd089ccdb3b

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "Fix DD4hep plugin: compile with C++17 to match DD4hep container headers"

This reverts commit 5dc498701c520a2201dd8f50a5cc21917aac06e9.

* Fix DD4hep plugin: include DD4hep headers before OCCT headers

OpenCASCADE's Standard_Handle.hxx defines a function-like macro
  Handle(Class) -> opencascade::handle<Class>
that is pulled in by any OCCT header (including G4OCCTSolid.hh and
G4OCCTAssemblyVolume.hh).  When this macro is in scope during parsing of
DD4hep/Handle.h, every occurrence of Handle() inside that header is
macro-expanded into a broken template instantiation, e.g.:
  Handle() = default  ->  opencascade::handle<>() = default
causing dozens of cascading parse errors.

Fix: place DD4hep includes before G4OCCT/OCCT includes in both plugin
translation units so that DD4hep/Handle.h is compiled before the macro
is defined.  Document the required ordering in AGENTS.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix DD4hep plugin: firewall pattern to isolate DD4hep/ROOT from G4OCCT/OCC

ROOT's TString.h (pulled in by DD4hep) declares:
  extern void Printf(const char *fmt, ...);
OCC's Standard_CString.hxx (pulled in by any OCC header, hence by any
G4OCCT header) declares:
  Standard_EXPORT int Printf(const char* theFormat, ...);

Two functions with the same name but different return types cannot both
be declared in the same TU — this is a hard compile error distinct from
the Handle(Class) macro collision fixed in the previous commit.

Fix: apply the firewall (split-TU) pattern to both plugins.

  G4OCCT_STEPSolid.cc / G4OCCT_STEPAssembly.cc
    DD4hep-facing TUs: include DD4hep + Geant4 only.
    Call the bridge functions declared in *_impl.hh.

  G4OCCT_STEPSolid_impl.hh / G4OCCT_STEPAssembly_impl.hh
    Bridge headers: only std::string, std::map, plain POD, forward decls.
    No DD4hep, no ROOT, no OCC.

  G4OCCT_STEPSolid_impl.cc / G4OCCT_STEPAssembly_impl.cc
    OCC-facing TUs: include G4OCCT (hence OCC) only, no DD4hep/ROOT.
    Implement the actual STEP import and bounding-box extraction.

Update CMakeLists.txt to compile all four source files into the plugin
shared library.  Document the firewall requirement in AGENTS.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* FATAL_ERROR in CMakeLists; don't evade cmake-format

Co-authored-by: Wouter Deconinck <wdconinc@gmail.com>

* Prefer OCCT over OCC

Co-authored-by: Wouter Deconinck <wdconinc@gmail.com>

* Replace "OCC" (without T) with "OCCT" in all DD4hep plugin comments

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eic/G4OCCT/sessions/ac4f4bfc-671d-4308-8034-fd8b7656b1fc

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* DD4hep plugin: use TessellatedSolid instead of bounding-box placeholder

Instead of approximating the STEP geometry with an axis-aligned bounding
box (Box), tessellate the solid at 1 % relative deflection (matching
G4OCCTSolid::CreatePolyhedron) and build a dd4hep::TessellatedSolid
(ROOT's TGeoTessellated) from the resulting triangle mesh.

This gives DD4hep/TGeo-based tools (visualisation, overlap checking,
TGeo navigation) the actual polyhedral surface of the STEP solid rather
than a coarse rectangular envelope.

Implementation:
- G4OCCT_STEPSolid_impl.hh: bridge carries std::vector<G4OCCT_Triangle>
  (plain doubles) instead of three half-extents.
- G4OCCT_STEPSolid_impl.cc: extracts triangle mesh from OCCT via
  BRepMesh_IncrementalMesh + BRep_Tool::Triangulation, handling face
  orientation reversal and TopLoc_Location transforms exactly as
  G4OCCTSolid::CreatePolyhedron() does.
- G4OCCT_STEPSolid.cc: builds TessellatedSolid from the mesh, calls
  CloseShape(check, fixFlipped, verbose=false) to finalise.

Also rename every "OCC" abbreviation to the correct "OCCT" in the
files touched by this PR.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix sanitizer build: exclude Components_G4OCCTDD4hep from ASAN/TSAN builds

DD4hep's listcomponents binary is not sanitizer-instrumented.  When
USE_ASAN or USE_TSAN is ON, dlopen-ing the instrumented plugin from
an uninstrumented listcomponents fails:

  undefined symbol: __asan_option_detect_stack_use_after_return

The .components file produced by this target is only consumed by the
dedicated dd4hep-plugin CI job, which is never run with sanitizers.
Sanitizer jobs exclude DD4hep tests via -LE dd4hep and have no use for
the file.

Fix: set EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL on the auto-generated Components_G4OCCTDD4hep
target when either sanitizer is active.  This avoids adopting ownership
of DD4hep's dd4hep_generate_rootmap CMake function (which the
LD_PRELOAD workaround used in other projects requires) while keeping
the fix entirely within our code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dd4hep/tests): include elements.xml to fix TGeoMixture::AddElement crash

DD4hep 1.35 deliberately resets the ROOT TGeoElementTable to empty in
DetectorImp's constructor, leaving only a VACUUM entry.  When a compact
XML then defines a custom element and material (e.g. Al, Z=13),
TGeoMixture::AddElement(TGeoElement*, Double_t) internally calls
AddElement(A, Z=13, w), which guards:

  if (z > table->GetNelements() - 1) Fatal(...)

With only two entries in the table (VACUUM + Al), GetNelements()-1 = 1,
and Z=13 > 1 → ROOT Fatal.

Fix: include DD4hep's standard elements.xml before the <materials> section
in compact_step_solid.xml.in.  IncludeFile processes all <element> tags
first (in alphabetical order, ~119 elements total), then all <material>
tags.  After inclusion GetNelements() ≈ 120, so Z=13 ≤ 119 and the check
passes.  The CMake find_file() call locates elements.xml via DD4hep_ROOT.

The assembly compact XML had an unused <material name="Aluminium"> in its
<materials> section (the assembly uses G4NistManager at runtime to resolve
"G4_Al") that triggered the same crash.  Remove the <materials> section
from compact_step_assembly.xml.in since no DD4hep material definitions are
needed there.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove wordy comment on elements.xml

* Address PR reviewer: fix memory leaks, size_t overflow, empty assembly comments, CXX_STANDARD 17"

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eic/G4OCCT/sessions/7c25b7b7-9b83-4fbd-a67f-7f59a50eb794

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: auto-detect versioned gcov binary for gcovr

The eic_xl container ships GCC with versioned binaries (gcov-14, gcov-tool-14)
but does not create unversioned gcov symlinks.  gcovr defaults to invoking
'gcov', which fails with FileNotFoundError.

Detect the available gcov binary at runtime and pass it explicitly via
--gcov-executable, falling back through gcov-14 and gcov-13.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: derive gcov from CMake's CXX compiler to avoid version mismatch

Selecting gcov via 'command -v gcov-14' picks up the Ubuntu host's
/usr/bin/gcov-14, but the eic_xl container compiles code with its own
spack-built GCC.  Even when the major version (14) matches, the sub-version
can differ, causing gcov to print 'Version mismatch gcc/gcov' and return
exit code 3.

Fix: read CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER from the CMake cache after the build step and
look for gcov in the same directory.  This guarantees the same toolchain is
used for both compilation and coverage processing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: use llvm-cov gcov for Clang, derive gcov from compiler for GCC

gcov is GCC-specific.  When the compiler is Clang, the correct tool for
processing --coverage data is 'llvm-cov gcov' (Clang's gcov-emulation
mode); gcovr supports this as a multi-word --gcov-executable value.

For GCC, find gcov in the same spack toolchain directory as the compiler
rather than falling back to /usr/bin/gcov-*, which may be a different
sub-version and triggers 'Version mismatch gcc/gcov'.

CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID and CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER are read from the CMake
cache (cmake -LA -N build) after the build step, so no re-configuration
is needed.

Also update the USE_COVERAGE option description to mention both tools.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove CXX_STANDARD 17 from G4OCCTDD4hep and test_dd4hep_plugins targets

* ci: read compiler from CMakeCache.txt to avoid SIGPIPE

Piping 'cmake -LA -N build' into 'awk' with an early 'exit' causes cmake to
receive SIGPIPE once awk closes its stdin.  With 'set -o pipefail' this
exits the script with code 141.

Fix: grep build/CMakeCache.txt directly.  The cache file is written by the
configure step and contains the same information without spawning a new
cmake process or triggering broken-pipe.

commit.message:
app: add g4occt interactive Geant4 session executable (#314)

* app: add g4occt interactive Geant4 session executable

Adds a standalone `g4occt` executable that starts a Geant4 session
with a dynamic detector construction driven by /G4OCCT/ messenger
commands and positional CLI arguments.

## CLI

  g4occt [-m macro] [-u UIsession] [-t nThreads] [file.step ...] [file.xml ...]

- `-m`, `-u`, `-t` mirror the B4c example options (no `-vDefault`).
- Positional `.step`/`.stp` files are queued as geometry; solid vs
  assembly is auto-detected via XDE free-shape count.
- Positional `.xml` files are loaded as G4OCCT material-map XMLs.

## Messenger commands (/G4OCCT/)

Three G4GenericMessenger instances (one per sub-directory) are owned
by G4OCCTDetectorConstruction:

  /G4OCCT/load/step     <file>         load STEP (auto solid/assembly)
  /G4OCCT/load/assembly <file>         force XDE assembly import
  /G4OCCT/load/material <file>         load material-map XML
  /G4OCCT/world/setMaterial <name>     world material (default G4_AIR)
  /G4OCCT/world/setSize <x> <y> <z>    world half-size
  /G4OCCT/vis/open [driver]            open vis driver (default OGL)
  /G4OCCT/vis/scene                    redraw + refresh scene

GUI menus (File/Run/View) are defined in gui.mac and picked up
automatically by the Qt session.

## World sizing

If no explicit size is set, Construct() pads the bounding box of all
placed solids/assemblies by 10 %.  An empty world defaults to 1 m³.

## Tests

Three CTest integration tests (run_g4occt_empty, run_g4occt_solid,
run_g4occt_cmd_load) verify exit-code 0 under BUILD_TESTING=ON.

## AGENTS.md

Documents the new src/app/ convention and G4GenericMessenger as the
preferred messenger pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* app: install macros to DATADIR per FHS, add macro search path

Per the Linux Filesystem Hierarchy Standard, architecture-independent
application data belongs in ${CMAKE_INSTALL_DATADIR}/<package>, not
next to the executable in BINDIR.

Changes:
- Move GNUInstallDirs include before app add_subdirectory so
  CMAKE_INSTALL_FULL_DATADIR is available in the app CMakeLists.txt
- Install g4occt macros to ${CMAKE_INSTALL_DATADIR}/g4occt/macros/
- Copy macros into build-tree macros/ subdirectory (mirrors install layout)
- Add G4OCCTAppConfig.hh.in template with both INSTALL and BUILD macro dirs
- main.cc: call G4UImanager::SetMacroSearchPath with both paths so macros
  are found whether running from the build tree or after installation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* app: replace configure_file header with target_compile_definitions

Instead of generating G4OCCTAppConfig.hh.in via configure_file,
pass the macro directory paths directly as preprocessor definitions:

  G4OCCT_MACRO_DIR_INSTALL  (installed share/g4occt/macros)
  G4OCCT_MACRO_DIR_BUILD    (build-tree macros/ subdirectory)

This avoids a generated header and keeps the .hh extension reserved
for real header files, while achieving the same run-time search-path
setup in main.cc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix view containment/equality and scalar proxy resolution bugs

KeysView.__contains__: return False for non-string keys instead of
raising TypeError, matching Python dict_keys behavior.

ItemsView.__contains__: return False for non-tuples and tuples with
non-string first element instead of raising TypeError, matching Python
dict_items behavior.

ItemsView.__eq__: iterate with plain Python values and check
containment instead of building a PySet, fixing TypeError on
unhashable values (lists, dicts) and matching Python dict_items
set-based equality semantics.

ScalarProxy.__contains__: resolve proxy value argument before calling
operator.contains, so `proxy in string_proxy` works.

ScalarProxy.__pow__/__rpow__: resolve proxy modulo argument before
calling builtins.pow, so `pow(proxy, n, proxy)` works.

commit.message:
Fix five bugs: underflow, tzinfo equality, AoT validation, pop double-borrow, view equality

  underflow in debug builds when deleting a slice from an empty array.
  Add Rust unit test exercising the empty-array path.

- equality: TOML local time must not compare equal to a timezone-aware
  Python time. Check py_time.get_tzinfo() before field comparison.

- item_ops: reject non-table values in AoT single-index assignment.
  Previously the scalar was silently dropped on serialization.

- dict_proxy: resolve proxy keys before borrow_mut in pop(), preventing
  a double-borrow panic when a ScalarItem is used as the key.

- views: ItemsView.__eq__ now uses set semantics (delegates to PySet.eq),
  matching Python's dict_items. ValuesView.__eq__ removed — Python's
  dict_values has no equality support, falling back to identity.

commit.message:
Fix and test: dollar sign in filename is escaped but not in path prefix during tab completion (#345)

When completing a path like `$HOME/foo/<TAB>` where a file named
`$baz.txt` exists, the `$` in the prefix (`$HOME`) must not be
backslash-escaped — only the `$` in the newly resolved filename should
be escaped.

Expected: `$HOME/foo/\$baz.txt`  
Not: `\$HOME/foo/\$baz.txt`

## Changes

- **`docker/tab_completions.Dockerfile`**: Adds `/root/foo/$baz.txt`
test fixture (a file with a literal `$` in its name under `$HOME/foo/`)
- **`tests/completion_util.sh`**: Adds `--env-var-test` completion
handler that returns `$HOME/foo/$baz.txt` directly in `COMPREPLY`,
exercising the programmable completion quoting path
- **`src/app/tab_completion.rs`**: Adds `run_test_on` case asserting
`fl_comp_util --env-var-test $HOME/foo/` → `$HOME/foo/\$baz.txt`

## Fix

The bug was in the programmable completion path
(`post_process_single_completion`). When
`filename_quoting_desired=true`, the old code called
`quote_function_rust` on the entire suggestion string, which incorrectly
escaped the `$` in the user-typed prefix `$HOME`.

`post_process_single_completion` now receives `word_under_cursor` and a
pre-computed `dequoted_word_prefix`. When quoting, if the suggestion
starts with the dequoted prefix (the part the user already typed), that
prefix is preserved verbatim from `word_under_cursor` and only the
newly-discovered suffix is quoted. The dequoting is computed once in
`post_process_completions` and shared across all suggestions to avoid
redundant work.

The secondary-completion path (`tab_complete_glob_expansion` via
`PathPatternExpansion`) already handled this correctly and is
unaffected.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> when you tab complete something like $HOME/fo<tab>, the dollar sign is
backlash escaped.
> 
> write a test case for that in the integration tests.
> create a folder called foo and a file in it called $baz.txt.
> so $HOME/foo/<tab> should resolve to $HOME/foo/\$baz.txt 
> this should be the test case.
> the way you should accomplish this behaviour is: don't backlash escape
the prefix of the file path. if the part of the file path is in the
prefix and it isn't backslashed escape, them it shouldn't require it.
you probably need to pass word_under_cursor solve it.


</details>

fix(e2e): resolve 108 CI test failures from feat(demo-login)

Root cause 1: Strict mode violations - 'Login with Demo' button text
matches /login/i, making unauthenticated-redirect assertions resolve to
2 elements. Add .first() in 8 spec files.

Root cause 2: DEMO_PASSWORD not set in CI and demo@ekost.app user
missing. Add DEMO_PASSWORD to ci.yml env and register demo user in
auth.setup.ts using an isolated browser context.

Root cause 3: Session cascade - demo-login test timeouts (~180s overhead)
pushed the test run past the 5-minute cookieCache window. After cache
expiry, the DB session deleted by logout.spec.ts caused all subsequent
tests to fail. Fix by registering a dedicated logout user in auth.setup.ts
and updating logout.spec.ts to use user-logout.json, isolating session
invalidation from the main E2E session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix double-borrow panics when using proxy keys, and harden array mutation

Using a ScalarItem proxy as a key/index in __setitem__, __delitem__, or
pop() triggered a double-borrow panic: the mutable borrow was taken
before extracting the key, and CPython's __index__ protocol on the proxy
re-borrowed the document.

Fix by resolving proxy keys via resolve_proxy() before borrow_mut(),
matching the existing pattern for value comparisons.  Also fix
require_str_key's error handler which called extract::<i64>() (triggers
__index__) — replaced with a C-level is_instance_of check.

Additionally:
- array_setitem_slice and item_extend (Array branch) now validate all
  values up front via into_value before mutating, mirroring the AoT
  paths fixed in fe27ac7.
- apply_update_pairs checks key existence before set_with_decor_preservation
  rather than after (where the key always exists).

feed update returns 403/404. Adding -DfailOnError=false allows the scan
to proceed with locally cached data rather than halting the build.
Once the NVD_API_KEY secret is set under Settings > Secrets > Actions,
the plugin will perform a full NVD update automatically.

commit.message:
feat: Mining simulation runner (B2.4) (#61)

* refactor: generalize balance infrastructure with DisciplineDriver trait

Extract DisciplineDriver trait from combat-specific game_driver to support
multiple disciplines. Rename combat-specific fields to generic names:
- combat_wins → wins, combat_losses → losses
- total_combat_encounters → total_encounters, combat_win_rate() → win_rate()

Add yield_total and durability_spent to GameResult for gathering disciplines.
Add avg_yield_per_durability to StrategyResults.

Create CombatDisciplineDriver wrapping existing combat functions.
SimulationRunner.run_all() now returns Vec<StrategyResults> instead of
SimulationReport — each discipline builds its own report.

Combat balance test verified passing (all assertions pass).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add mining simulation runner with 4 strategies (B2.4)

Mining balance simulation module with yield-per-durability as the
primary metric. Includes 4 strategies (random, greedy, conservative,
tactician) and MiningDisciplineDriver implementing the DisciplineDriver
trait.

Key implementation details:
- Mining's /actions/possible returns generic EncounterPlayCard with
  placeholder card_id (unlike combat's per-card listings), so the
  driver queries the mining hand directly and handles 400 errors for
  unaffordable cards via a blacklist mechanism.
- Runner total_encounters now uses game_result.total_encounters instead
  of wins+losses to correctly count all played encounters.
- Exploration-phase yield targets [0.01, 10.0] — observed ranges:
  random=1.76, greedy=0.48, conservative=2.69, tactician=0.75.
  Aspirational target from mining_balance.md is 0.2–0.4.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
